title: Renewable Energy Expo India 2023
content_html:
D

Su-vastika is participating in Renewable Energy Storage Expo 2023, held in Greater Noida and displaying a new product range for the first time.https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BTmMXV8LGuI&t=42s

 

The Renewable Energy India Expo 2023 was held from October 4-6, 2023, at the India Expo Centre and Mart in Greater Noida, India [1]. It is the largest trade expo in the Indian market for renewable energy and is known as Asia’s Leading b2b expo focusing on Solar Energy, Wind Energy, Bio-Energy, Energy Storage and Electric Vehicles and charging infra.

Here are some of the highlights of the event:

  • Over 43,000 trade visitors attended the expo.
  • There were more than 621 exhibitors and 801 brands showcasing their products and services.
  • The event featured a conference with over 270 distinguished speakers who addressed policy initiatives and innovative finance models for the renewable energy sector.

The expo is a key platform for businesses, academic institutions, and the government to explore renewable energy resources and to mainstream them as a means to meet the world’s growing demands for clean energy

The Renewable Energy Expo India 2023 has already concluded, taking place in October 2023. However, if you’re interested in participating in future exhibitions, here’s some general information regarding eligibility:

Target Audience (Likely):

The expo is aimed at a broad range of participants in the renewable energy sector, including:

  • Manufacturers: Companies that produce solar panels, wind turbines, bioenergy equipment, energy storage solutions, electric vehicles, and charging infrastructure.
  • Suppliers: Those involved in supplying components, materials, and services related to renewable energy technologies.
  • Project Developers: Companies and organizations developing and implementing renewable energy projects.
  • Government Agencies: Representatives from government agencies responsible for promoting and regulating renewable energy.
  • Utilities: Representatives from power utilities and distribution companies interested in renewable energy solutions.
  • Investors: Individuals and organizations seeking investment opportunities in the renewable energy sector.
  • Consultants and Service Providers: Companies offering consulting services, legal services, or other expertise relevant to the renewable energy sector.

Participation Options (Likely):

There are several ways to participate in the expo, depending on your goals:

  • Exhibition Booth: Renting a booth to showcase your company’s products or services related to the renewable energy sector. This is likely the most common way for businesses to participate.
  • Conference Sponsorship: Sponsoring various aspects of the conference to gain visibility and brand recognition.
  • Speaker Opportunities: Submitting proposals to speak at the conference on relevant topics.
  • Visitor Attendance: Attending the expo to network with other participants, learn about the latest advancements, and explore potential business opportunities.

Who Might Not Be Eligible:

While there are no strict eligibility requirements, it wouldn’t be the most relevant platform for businesses or individuals not associated with the renewable energy sector.

Looking Ahead:

For information about eligibility and participation in future Renewable Energy Expo India events, here are some resources:

  • Official Website of the Expo: The official website likely has a dedicated section for exhibitors or might mention contact details for inquiries regarding participation. Search for “Renewable Energy Expo India” or the organizer’s name to find the relevant website.
  • News and Announcements: Stay updated on news articles and announcements related to the expo. Information regarding future events, participation opportunities, and eligibility criteria might be announced through these channels.

By following these suggestions, you can stay informed about eligibility and how to participate in future Renewable Energy Expo India events.

title: Nigeria Energy 2023
content_html:
Nigeria Energy 2023 was an annual event held from September 19-21, 2023, at the Landmark Centre in Lagos, Nigeria. It was the 10th edition of this even which has become a key meeting point for stakeholders in Nigeria’s energy sector.

The event brought together key stakeholders and government representatives with visionary professionals to find solutions for Nigeria’s peak energy demand and to pave the way for decentralization of the energy sector. The goal of the even was to accelerate Nigeria’s sustainable energy supply.

The conferences featured high-level discussions and a large exhibition showcasing the latest advancements in the energy sector [1]. The focus was on finding a blueprint for Nigeria’s power sector in the coming years with the aim of improving access to electricity, driving economic growth, and creating jobs throughout West Africa.

Since Nigeria Energy 2023 took place in September 2023, it’s no longer possible to participate in that specific event. However, if you’re interested in future Nigeria Energy events, here’s some information that might be helpful regarding potential eligibility:

Target Audience (Likely):

  • Energy Sector Professionals: This includes representatives from power generation companies, transmission and distribution companies, renewable energy companies, oil and gas companies, and engineering firms involved in the energy sector.
  • Government Officials: Representatives from the Ministry of Power, Ministry of Petroleum Resources, and other relevant government agencies involved in energy policy and regulation.
  • Investors and Financiers: Those interested in investing in Nigeria’s energy sector projects.
  • Consultants and Service Providers: Companies offering consulting services, legal services, or other expertise relevant to the energy sector.
  • Technology Providers: Companies showcasing innovative technologies for power generation, transmission, distribution, and energy efficiency.

Ways to Participate (Likely):

  • Conference Attendance: Registering to attend the conference sessions and panel discussions.
  • Exhibition Booth: Renting a booth to showcase your company’s products or services related to the energy sector.
  • Sponsorship Opportunities: Sponsoring various aspects of the event to gain visibility and brand recognition.
  • Speaker Opportunities: Submitting proposals to speak at the conference on relevant topics.

Looking Ahead:

While specific details for future events might not be available yet, here are some resources to keep an eye on for information on eligibility and participation in Nigeria Energy events:

  • Nigeria Energy Website (if available): There might be an official website for the event that gets updated with information for future editions. Search for “Nigeria Energy” or the organizer’s name to find the relevant website.
  • Industry News and Publications: Stay updated on industry publications and news websites that cover events related to Nigeria’s energy sector. These sources might announce details about future Nigeria Energy events and participation opportunities.

By following these resources, you can stay informed about eligibility requirements and how to participate in future Nigeria Energy events.

 

 

 

 

 

Nigeria Energy, formerly Power Nigeria, exhibition and conference is the leading energy event in West Africa. After nine successful annual editions, Nigeria Energy is set to return for its 10th-anniversary edition, bigger and better. Held under the patronage of Nigeria’s Ministry of Power, the exhibition and conference attracted hundreds of local and international suppliers and thousands of professional visitors.

Nigeria Energy caters to the entire value chain, from manufacturers and global industry experts to governments and power industry professionals. Public utilities can meet with consultants, EPC contractors and technology providers to form partnerships and provide solutions for the challenges facing the region’s energy sector.

The event provides a unique platform for exhibitors worldwide to showcase cutting-edge products and services. The exhibition provides an opportunity for buyers and sellers from across the globe to develop international relationships and for investors to discover the opportunities available in the region. Exhibitors and attendees can network and learn through the content-rich conference programme about technology, trends and emerging themes from the public and private sectors. An increasing drive for clean energy to supplement the growing demand for conventional power capacity is emerging as a key theme across Africa’s utilities sectors.

Su-vastika took part in Nigeria Energy 2023 and was represented by Mr. kunwer Sachdev Foundert Su-kam and Mentor Su-vastika. He showed the Lithium Inverter range along with the Battery Energy Storage Systems and drew much interest among the African continent.

Lithium Inbuilt ESS

The inbuilt lithium ESS (Energy Storage System) is a cutting-edge power solution designed for various applications. It utilizes Pure Sine Wave Technology, ensuring compatibility with IT loads and sensitive appliances. With fast charging capabilities and a high-efficiency LifePO4 battery, it provides reliable and uninterrupted power supply.

Battery Backup and Power Storage Solutions

Whether in domestic or commercial space, power cuts have become a reality in some developing countries. To curb this, we are making storage solutions with real-time monitoring systems and the latest lithium Battery technology. The UPS/ inverter range includes a single phase of up to 15 KVA; for the 3 phases, the range is up to 250 KVA.

We have designed an Automatic Testing Setup (ATS). ATS aids in high-quality manufacturing and testing of UPS/Inverters, solar inverters, on-grid and off-grid, and hybrid solar inverters for single-phase and 3-phase technology.

Our Automatic Testing set-up includes:

  • Electrical vehicle testing machines for the manufacturing of 2-wheelers and 3-wheelers as well as for repairing these EVs
  • Lithium battery testing machines for testing the BMS (Battery Management Solution) as well as testing the whole lithium bank from 12 V to 600 V DC bank
  • Functional PCB Testing machines that are universal in nature. All these machines are developed on online monitoring solutions so that one can see the data online and store the important information for future traceability

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=’suvastika_text’ template_class=” element_template=” one_element_template=” av_uid=’av-lw8szgbf’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]
Thank you for choosing Su-vastika as your trusted partner in providing top-notch inverters, UPS and Energy Storage solutions. We understand and respect your privacy rights, and we are committed to protecting your personal information. This Privacy Policy outlines how we collect, use, disclose, and protect your information when you interact with our products, services, websites, and applications.

1. Information We Collect

1.1. Personal Information: When you engage with Su-vastika’s products and services, we may collect personal information such as your name, contact details, email address, shipping address, and billing information.

1.2. Usage Information: We collect information about your interactions with our websites, applications, and services, including your IP address, device information, browser type, pages visited, and access times.

1.3. Cookies and Similar Technologies: We may use cookies and similar technologies to enhance your experience, personalize content, analyze trends, and administer our websites. You can manage your cookie preferences through your browser settings.

2. How We Use Your Information

2.1. Providing Services: We use your personal information to deliver the products, services, and support you request, including processing orders, providing technical assistance, and responding to inquiries.

2.2. Improving Experiences: We analyze usage data to understand customer preferences, optimize product performance, and enhance user experiences.

2.3. Communications: We may send you important updates, announcements, and promotional offers related to our products and services. You can opt out of marketing communications at any time.

3. Information Sharing

3.1. Third-Party Service Providers: We may share your information with trusted third-party service providers to facilitate transactions, deliver services, or perform functions on our behalf. These providers are contractually obligated to maintain the confidentiality and security of your information.

3.2. Legal Compliance: We may disclose your information to comply with applicable laws, regulations, legal processes, or governmental requests.

4. Data Security

4.1. Security Measures: We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information from unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction.

4.2. Data Retention: We retain your information only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes outlined in this Privacy Policy, unless a longer retention period is required by law.

5. Your Privacy Choices

5.1. Access and Correction: You may review, update, or correct your personal information by contacting us directly.

5.2. Opt-Out: You have the right to opt out of certain data processing activities, such as marketing communications. Please contact us to exercise your preferences.

6. Children’s Privacy

Su-vastika’s products and services are not directed towards individuals under the age of 18. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children without parental consent.

7. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices or applicable laws. We will notify you of any material updates by posting the revised policy on our website or through other appropriate channels.

8. Contact Us

If you have any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us at info@suvastika.com / +91 9711774744.

By using Su-vastika’s products and services, you consent to the collection, use, and disclosure of your information as described in this Privacy Policy.

9. Account Deletion

If a user wishes to delete their account, they can do so by logging into their account. We provide an account deletion option within the user settings. By using this option, the user can permanently delete their account, which will result in the removal of all associated data and revoke their access to the portal.

Please note that once the account is deleted, this action is irreversible, and the user will no longer be able to recover any information.
[/av_textblock]

[av_heading heading=’What is Design patent and what are the benefits ‘ tag=’h1′ style=’blockquote modern-quote modern-centered’ subheading_active=” show_icon=” icon=’ue800′ font=’entypo-fontello’ size=’38’ av-medium-font-size-title=” av-small-font-size-title=” av-mini-font-size-title=” subheading_size=’15’ av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” icon_size=” av-medium-font-size-1=” av-small-font-size-1=” av-mini-font-size-1=” color=” custom_font=” subheading_color=” seperator_color=” icon_color=” margin=” margin_sync=’true’ padding=’10’ icon_padding=’10’ headline_padding=” headline_padding_sync=’true’ link=’manually,https://suvastika.com/technology-patents-su-vastika-11-numbers-solar-battery-inverter-energy-storage-system/’ link_target=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” element_template=” one_element_template=” av_uid=’av-l8o2a88r’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]
A design patent is a type of intellectual property (IP) protection that gives the patent holder the exclusive right to use the design of an article for a period of 10 years. In India, design patents are governed by the Designs Act, 2000.

To be eligible for a design patent, the design must be new and original. It must also be applicable to an article of manufacture. The article of manufacture can be a product, a part of a product, or a combination of products.

The benefits of obtaining a design patent in India include:

The exclusive right to use the design for a period of 10 years.
The ability to prevent others from making, using, selling, or importing products that infringe the design.
The ability to use the design patent as a marketing tool to show that the product is innovative and unique.
The process of obtaining a design patent in India is relatively straightforward. The first step is to file a design patent application with the Indian Patent Office. The application must include a drawing of the design and a description of the design.

The Indian Patent Office will then examine the application to determine if it meets the requirements for a design patent. If the application is approved, the design patent will be granted. The design patent will be published in the Indian Patent Journal.

The benefits of obtaining a design patent in India can be significant. If you have a unique and innovative design for a product, a design patent can help you protect your investment and prevent others from copying your design.

Here are some additional details about design patents in India:

The design must be new and original. This means that it must not have been published or used anywhere in the world before the date of the application.
The design must be applicable to an article of manufacture. This means that it must be a design for a physical object that can be manufactured.
The design patent will give you the exclusive right to use the design in India for a period of 10 years.
You can file a design patent application at any of the four patent offices in India: Delhi, Mumbai, Chennai, or Kolkata.
The Indian Patent Office will examine your application to determine if it meets the requirements for a design patent.
If your application is approved, you will be granted a design patent.
Your design patent will be published in the Indian Patent Journal.
At Su-vastika we already have got 20 design patents with us for our various product range like Inverter/UPS/Solar inverter/Industrial UPS/Lift/Lithium battery packs/lift UPS/ESS etc. We continuously filing our design patents
[/av_heading]

The MEE Exhibition you’re referring to likely took place in March 2023, since today is March 12, 2here in 2024. Here’s what I can tell you about the Middle East Energy Exhibition 2023:

It happened: The 48th edition of the Middle East Energy Exhibition (MEE) was held between March 6th and March 8th, 2023, at the Dubai World Trade Centre.

Focus of the event: MEE is a well-established event known for bringing together energy professionals from all over the world [5]. Key areas of focus include:

  • Renewable Energy
  • Transmission & Distribution
  • Energy Consumption & Management
  • Backup Generators & Critical Power
  • Smart Energy Solutions

Benefits of attending (for 2023):

  • Networking opportunities: Connecting with industry leaders, potential business partners, and stakeholders in the energy sector from across the globe.
  • Discovering new technologies: Showcasing cutting-edge advancements and innovations in the energy sector from a wide range of exhibitors.
  • Staying updated on trends: Gaining insights into the latest trends and developments shaping the future of the energy industry through conferences and presentations.

Resources to learn more (about 2023):

  • Official Website (for past events): While the information might not be live anymore, you can visit the Middle East Energy website to get a sense of the event’s scope and the types of companies that participate https://www.middleeast-energy.com/en/home.html.
  • News Articles: Searching online for news articles about “Middle East Energy Exhibition 2023” might reveal details about the specific event that happened in March 2023.

Looking ahead (for 2024):

The next Middle East Energy Exhibition is scheduled for April 16th to 18th, 2024. If you’re interested in attending the upcoming event, you can visit the official website for more information on registration, exhibitors, and the program schedule.

Su-vastika Systems Private Limited  received a fantastic response at the 48th edition of Middle East Energy (MEE) in Dubai. From Mar 7th to 9th 2023, Su-vastika captured the attention of the world with its Wi-fi and Bluetooth monitored Energy Storage Systems.

In this exhibition Su-vastika showed Lithium in built Inverters/UPS for the first time and Pure Sinewave UPS having Bluetooth and Wi-Fi based applications. Showing the Battery Energy storage c. range up to 250KVA having Lithium Battery bank and having Solar Storage system upgradation, Lift Inverters/ERD and other power backup and storage solutions. Su-vastika is the first ompany in power backup and solar storage solutions to get the BIS and CE certification.
[/av_textblock]

Lift Inverter/ERD/Lift backup

Lift Inverter/ERD is an Inverter/UPS that acts during power failures and gives backup uninterruptedly and no jerk is felt by the passengers travelling in the lift. In the event that the main power source fails, this emergency equipment supplies a 3-phase power supply to the Lifts and Elevators.

[/av_textblock]

title: PCB Functional Tester
content_html:
[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” av_uid=’av-lfx0kkj1′ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]

Universal PCB Functional Tester

A functional PCB tester is a critical machine used in electronics manufacturing that ensures the quality and reliability of populated printed circuit boards.https://suvastika.com/ats-for-pcb-testing-a-revolutionary-software-by-su-vastika-to-check-tp-points-of-pcb/

It performs automated tests to verify the electrical connections, components, and features/functionally of the populated  PCB and helps manufacturers maintain consistent quality across different batches of products.

The PCB Functional testing machine consists of a hardware platform that includes test probes, switches, and other measurement instruments and a software system that controls the test sequence and records the test results.

The software is usually designed to be highly customizable, allowing manufacturers to define their tests and specifications and to add new tests as needed.

PCB functional testing machines are essential for ensuring the quality and reliability of electronic products because catching defects early in the manufacturing process can save manufacturers time and money by preventing costly rework in the field or product recalls which can impact reputation and future sales.

They are also essential for maintaining consistent quality across different batches of PCBs, ensuring that products meet the required standards and specifications even if some components change across clusters.

There are many functional testers available in the market. Some functional PCB testers are designed to test the PCB’s functionality and program the microcontroller on the PCB.

These testers are often equipped with programming tools, such as an ISP (in-system programming) or JTAG (Joint Test Action Group) interface, to program the microcontroller directly on the PCB. Suvastika’s PCB Functional Tester has this capability inbuilt.

However, not all functional PCB testers are designed to program microcontrollers. Some testers may only be capable of testing the functionality of the PCB and not programming the microcontroller. In this case, a separate programming tool would need to program the microcontroller, which is an additional step and hassle in production.

It’s essential to check the specifications of the specific functional PCB tester you are thinking of using to determine if it can program the microcontroller on the PCB.In-circuit testing – Wikipedia

Salient Features:

  • Fully Automatic PCB Functional Testing machine  with cloud  connectivity
  • Modular Jig fixture to handle different PCB variant
  • The standard jig can support up to 40 test points and can be expanded easily
  • Can check AC/DC voltage, Current, Resistance, etc., for each test point
  • Automated MCU firmware Programming and verification
  • Automated calibration of parameters
  • UART interface for protocol-level testing or board configuration
  • PC Interface: 2x USB 2.0
  • Operating voltage 230V, 50/60Hz
  • Weight: 12-15 Kg, depending on the instrumentation
  • Dimensions: 18 inches (L) x 18 inches (B) x 25 inches (H)
  • Operating Temperature: 0 – 45 Degrees C
  • PC Software
  • Operator Access Control
  • Testing Jig Configuration
  • Test Program Creation and modification
  • Thermal printer Interface for printing QR code stickers (PASS, FAIL)
  • Test data is stored in the local database as well as synched with the Cloud
  • Smart Factory Cloud Platform to monitor PCB Functional testing Remotely
  • Designed and manufactured in India
  • Customization is possible on request

Suvastika is also offering PCB Functional Testing on a Subscription basis which is the first time any company is offering it as an enterprise solution.

Application of PCB Functional Tester:

  • Automated Production testing of analogue/digital electronics assemblies (PCB tests). Functional testing for products like Inverter/UPS, BMS, sensors, Dataloggers, Controller cards, display cards, and any PCB needs functional testing.
  • Alternative to expensive high-end testers
  • Automation of manual tests in the R&D and Validation department.
  • The repair departments in the factory can use PCB functional tester to quickly find issues instead of debugging the board manually by spending hours.

Parts of PCB Functional Tester

There are three main parts in a PCB Functional Testing Setup

  1. Testing Jig
  2. PC Software
  3. Smart Factory Cloud Platform

For PCB Functional Testing, one needs PCB Function testing Jig, A PC, and software, and if remote monitoring is required, then a cloud-based intelligent factory app.

Windows PC software allows one to configure the PCB functional tester for any PCB with any number of test points, and you can set how many points to be tested, the maximum, and minimum voltage/current/resistance value, how many samples need to be used for averaging, etc.

Operator/Admin access control can be provided using ID/ password or QR code scanning.

One can save different test programs for each PCB or its variant and then can select to start using it for the test quickly.

While running, PC software shows the testing progress and status of each test. Each test point measurement is compared with its configured min and maximum value, and the result is displayed as PASS or FAIL, along with the actual measured value for that particular test point.

In the image above, the right-hand side panel is the terminal view, which allows an operator or floor supervisor to see the progress in more detail. This helps quickly find the issue in case PCB functional tester is not working or misbehaving.

PC software automatically generates the serial number based on Date, Time, Factory Code, Jig Code, and serial number, which helps one to identify the PCB in its complete lifetime.

QR code will be posted on the PCB once testing is completed.

Test result data is stored in the local database and pushed to Suvastika’s Smart Factory cloud platform, where Factory managers can easily monitor the testing progress and the result.

Suvastika’s Smart Factory Cloud App

Cloud Platform Login: This is the log page of Suvastika’s Smart Factory Cloud Platform. One can log in using an authorized email id and password.

List of different variants of PCBs tested (in the selected duration)

Once login is successful, you can see all other variants of PCBs getting tested and their pass-fail count with percentage. This gives a perfect overview to the factory manager.

One can change the period to see the status during that period, like if you want to see which PCBs got tested last month.

 

List of all Serial numbers of a particular PCB variant

If the Factory manager wants to see details on a specific variant of the PCB that is getting tested, the eye icon can be clicked, and the screen below will open where the list of PCB with their serial number and other information like test duration, who is the test engineer, pass/fail status are listed.

Detailed view showing each test point

Factory managers can go into further detail by clicking on any serial number, and then the screen, as shown, will be displayed. Here you see a list of all tests carried out on the PCB and its result.

This helps the factory manager see what’s going on in minute detail and gives complete control.

Production Trend with Pass, Fail

For quick analysis, Suvastika’s innovative factory platform provides a trend view where the factory manager can see how production progressed for a period, like in this month so far or last month, or during the selected period, which could be the last 6/12 months.

This view is critical to see if production is improving and if failures are getting controlled after specific changes made by the team. Etc.

The best part is no one needs to take manual reading to do this analysis. Everything is automated and manipulation-proof.

Suvastika’s team is constantly working on improving the features of its PCB Functional Tester, its associated PC software, and the Smart Factory Cloud Platform.

[/av_textblock]

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” element_template=” one_element_template=” av_uid=’av-l8xz596w’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]

 First Indian Company Awarded CE Mark for Power Backup Industry as this certificate allows Su-vastika to export its products to European countries.  Su-vastika Systems Private Limited was the first company in India to receive the CE certificate for its Energy Storage Systems (ESS), Lithium Inverters, Inverter, UPS Lithium battery Lift Inverters, and Solar PCUs. This is a significant achievement for the company, as the CE marking signifies compliance with European Union safety, health, and environmental standards.

CE marking is not mandatory for power backup products sold within India. Instead, the mandatory certification for such products is the Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) certification. The BIS mark indicates that the product has met the safety and performance standards set by the Indian government.

However, CE marking can be important for power backup manufacturers in India for the following reasons:

Exporting to the European Union (EU): CE marking is mandatory for power backup products sold in the EU market. Obtaining a CE marking demonstrates that the product complies with relevant EU health, safety, and environmental regulations. This is essential for manufacturers who want to export their products to the EU.

Enhancing brand image: Even though not mandatory within India, having CE marking can be seen as a mark of quality and safety for consumers. This can help manufacturers in India to differentiate their products from the competition and potentially command a higher price premium.

Here’s some additional information about Su-vastika’s achievement:

  • Scope of the CE certificate: The certificate covers a wide range of Su-vastika’s products, including various models of ESS, Lithium Inverters, Lift Inverters, and Solar PCUs. This demonstrates the company’s commitment to adhering to stringent quality and safety standards across its product line.
  • Importance of the CE marking: The CE marking is a recognized symbol of quality and safety in the European market. This certification allows Su-vastika to export its products to European countries and expand its global reach.
  • Su-vastika’s dedication to innovation: This achievement highlights Su-vastika’s commitment to innovation and technological advancement. By developing high-quality products that meet international standards, the company is positioning itself as a leader in the Indian renewable energy and power backup solutions market.

Overall, Su-vastika’s accomplishment in receiving the CE certificate is a significant milestone for the company and the Indian renewable energy sector. Su-vastika is the First Indian Company Awarded CE Mark for Power Backup Industry. It demonstrates the growing potential of Indian companies to compete in the global market with innovative and high-quality products.

[/av_textblock]

[/av_two_third][/av_section]

title: Customer Experience
content_html:
Clean Energy Revolution: Petrol Pump Replaces DG with Su-vastika Battery System"

The video showcases the Su-vastika Energy Storage System (ESS) as a replacement for diesel generators (DGs) at a petrol pump in Gurgaon. The petrol pump owner installed a Su-vastika ESS with a lithium battery to replace the DG. This system powers the entire petrol pump, including AC, lighting, fuel nozzles and a compressor, ensuring continuous operation during power cuts with an automatic changeover in two milliseconds. The petrol pump owner confirms that the system works perfectly and they don’t even notice when the power goes out.

"Silent Revolution: Hotel Replaces Noisy Generator with Clean Energy Solution"

This project showcases the successful transition of the Classic Diplomat Hotel in Delhi from a noisy, polluting diesel generator to a silent, efficient lithium-based Energy Storage System (ESS). By replacing their 325 KVA generator with a 200 KVA lithium ESS, the hotel has achieved seamless power backup, eliminating disruptions and improving the guest experience. The project highlights the benefits of lithium ESS, including instant power transition, silent operation, and reduced environmental impact, demonstrating a significant step towards sustainable energy solutions for commercial applications.

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

Beyond Diesel: Hospitals Embrace Lithium ESS for Reliable Power

 

Hospitals, with their critical need for uninterrupted power, have traditionally relied on diesel generators for backup. However, these generators come with a host of drawbacks: they’re noisy, polluting, require significant space, and have high running costs. Furthermore, the 2-3 minute delay in startup can be detrimental in emergency situations. This video explores a superior alternative: the lithium energy storage system (ESS), specifically Su-vastika’s 50 KVA solution.  

 

The video meticulously outlines the problems associated with diesel generators. From the space constraints and fuel dependency to the environmental impact and security risks, it paints a clear picture of their limitations. In contrast, the lithium ESS emerges as a clean, efficient, and reliable solution.

Su-vastika’s 50 KVA lithium ESS, showcased in the video, offers a compelling alternative. This system, comprising an inverter and lithium batteries, is designed for seamless power backup. Unlike diesel generators, it’s compact, requires no fuel, and operates silently. The key advantage lies in its near-instantaneous switchover time of just 2 milliseconds, ensuring uninterrupted power supply to critical hospital equipment.  

 
 

The video further emphasizes the system’s efficiency by demonstrating that a 50 KVA lithium ESS can effectively replace a 125 KVA diesel generator, highlighting its superior power management capabilities. The system’s maintenance-free operation and long lifespan (up to 10 years for the batteries) add to its appeal.

Real-world testimonials from hospital staff and owners further validate the benefits of the lithium ESS. They highlight its ease of use, reliability during power outages, and cost-effectiveness. The hospital owner, in particular, emphasizes the critical importance of a reliable power backup system in a medical facility and expresses satisfaction with the lithium batteries’ performance.

This video effectively presents the lithium ESS as a game-changer for hospitals and similar critical infrastructure. By eliminating the drawbacks of diesel generators, it offers a cleaner, more efficient, and reliable power backup solution, ensuring uninterrupted operations and peace of mind.

Connecting Emotionally with Su-vastika's Energy Solutions:

 

 

Imagine the peace of mind that comes with knowing your home or business remains powered, regardless of external disruptions. It’s more than just having electricity; it’s about maintaining a sense of normalcy and security.

Here’s how we can connect emotionally:

  • The Relief of Uninterrupted Life:
    • “Before Su-vastika, power cuts meant halting everything. Now, my children can continue their online classes, and I can work without interruption. It’s like the grid never went down,” one homeowner might say. This highlights the emotional relief of maintaining a normal routine.
    • “For my business, downtime means lost revenue. With Su-vastika, I’ve eliminated that anxiety. My customers are happy, and so am I.” This emphasizes the emotional security of business continuity.
  • The Comfort of Consistent Comfort:
    • “Living in a hot climate, air conditioning is essential. Su-vastika ensures my family stays comfortable, even during extended outages. That’s invaluable,” a resident could express. This underlines the emotional comfort of a stable living environment.
  • The Pride of Sustainable Choices:
    • “Knowing I’m reducing my carbon footprint gives me a sense of pride. I’m doing my part for the environment, and I’m not sacrificing reliability,” an environmentally conscious owner might state. This connects to the positive emotions associated with eco-friendly choices.
  • The Security of Reliable Operation:
    • “In a hospital setting, reliable power is critical. Su-vastika gives us the confidence to focus on patient care, knowing the lights and critical equipment will stay on.” This shows the emotional security provided in critical applications.
  • The end of the diesel generator stress:
    • “I used to hate the smell and the noise of the old diesel generator. Now, it is so quite, and clean. It is like a breath of fresh air.” This quote shows the relief of removing the negative aspects of older technology.

 

Solar Power for Your Home 5.5KVA MPPT Solar PCU

Empower your home with the future of clean energy! Say goodbye to the noise, fumes, and maintenance headaches of traditional generators and embrace the power of the sun with Su-vastika’s 5kVA MPPT solar system. Our compact and space-saving system seamlessly integrates into your home, providing reliable and uninterrupted power for all your needs. With remote monitoring capabilities, you can keep track of your energy usage and ensure that your power supply is always running smoothly. Experience the peace of mind that comes from knowing you’re powered by the sun, reducing your carbon footprint and saving money on energy costs. Embrace the future of sustainable living and choose Su-vastika for a cleaner, greener, and more reliable power solution.

Su-vastika Pioneers 75KVA Lithium Battery System Installation in Gurgaon Office

 

Su-vastika has marked a significant milestone by deploying one of the first 75KVA lithium battery energy storage systems in a commercial office space in Gurgaon, India. This installation represents a leap forward in providing reliable and sustainable backup power solutions for businesses.

Key highlights of this achievement include:

  • Replacing Traditional DG Sets:
    • The lithium-based energy storage system serves as a modern and efficient alternative to traditional diesel generator (DG) sets, addressing concerns related to noise pollution, emissions, and high operational costs.  
       
  • Advanced Lithium Battery Technology:
    • Su-vastika’s system utilizes advanced lithium battery technology, offering benefits such as longer lifespan, faster charging capabilities, and a smaller footprint compared to conventional lead-acid batteries.
  • Enhanced Reliability and Efficiency:
    • The system ensures seamless power backup, minimizing downtime and safeguarding critical operations for businesses reliant on uninterrupted power supply.
  • Smart Monitoring and Management:
    • Su-vastika integrates sophisticated monitoring systems, including Wi-Fi connectivity, enabling users to remotely track and manage the energy storage system’s performance.  
       
  • Commitment to Sustainable Energy:
    • This installation underscores Su-vastika’s dedication to promoting clean and sustainable energy solutions, contributing to a greener and more environmentally conscious future.

This pioneering installation in Gurgaon highlights Su-vastika’s role as a leading innovator in the energy storage sector, providing businesses with reliable, efficient, and environmentally friendly power backup solutions.

 
 

5.5KVA Solar MPPT Inverter with Lithium Battery replacing Lead Acid Battery System

The video showcases a user’s positive experience with a Su-vastika lithium battery solar inverter system installed in their home.

Here’s a summary:

  • Reliable Power: The system provides consistent backup, eliminating power issues and handling grid fluctuations [00:17].
  • Appliance Compatibility: It efficiently powers heavy appliances like washing machines, AC units, and induction cooktops [00:37].
  • Cost Savings: The user notes a significant decrease in their electricity bill [00:54], thanks to the efficient lithium batteries [01:29].
  • Low Maintenance: The system is maintenance-free, unlike lead-acid batteries [02:14].
  • Smart Monitoring: A mobile app allows users to monitor system parameters and warranty information [03:30], [04:05].
  • Customizable: Su-vastika offers customized systems for various needs [04:45].
  • System Details: The user’s system includes a 4.8 kWh lithium battery and a solar hybrid unit supporting up to 4kW of solar panels [05:21].

Electrical Contractor License For Su-vastika is necessary for installing Lift Inverters/Lift UPS/ERD.https://suvastika.com/whats-lift-inverter-and-compare-with-lift-ups/.

Electrical Contractor License For Su-vastika has major benefits for Su-vastika as installing lift Inverters of 3 phases we need qualified engineers to do the job.

There are many benefits to obtaining an electrical contractor certificate in India. Some of the key benefits include:

  • Legal authorization to carry out electrical work: An electrical contractor certificate is required to carry out electrical work in Haryana legally. This means that you will be able to bid on government contracts and work on commercial projects.
  • Increased credibility and trust: An electrical contractor certificate shows you have the necessary skills and experience to carry out electrical work safely and competently. This can help you to attract more customers and build your business.
  • Access to government contracts: Many government contracts require that electrical contractors have an electrical contractor certificate. You can compete for a wider range of projects with a certificate.
  • Reduced liability risks: An electrical contractor certificate can help to reduce your liability risks. This is because it shows that you have taken the necessary steps to ensure your work is safe and compliant with the law.
  • Improved safety standards: An electrical contractor certificate requires you to follow certain safety standards. This can help ensure your work is safe for you and your customers.

Overall, there are many benefits to obtaining an electrical contractor certificate in Haryana. If you are considering a career in electrical contracting, I recommend getting a certificate. It can help you build your business, attract customers, and reduce liability risks.

Here are some additional benefits of obtaining an electrical contractor certificate in Haryana:

  • Increased job opportunities: The demand for electrical contractors is growing in Haryana. This means that there are more job opportunities available for qualified electrical contractors.
  • Higher earnings: Electrical contractors with a certificate typically earn higher wages than those without a certificate.
  • Professional development: Obtaining an electrical contractor certificate can help you to develop your professional skills and knowledge. This can help you to advance your career and become a more successful electrical contractor.

An electrical contractor certificate is a requirement by any State govt for the safety standard of Lifts installation.

Su-vastika Systems Pvt. Ltd. received the permission for Export Cargo by The Principal Commissioner Of Customs (Preventive).

The term “Indian Custom Certificate” isn’t a single specific document used by Indian Customs. However, there are a couple of possibilities depending on the context:

  • Export Certificate: This is issued by Indian Customs to passengers leaving India with high-value personal items like jewelry, cameras, or expensive electronics. The certificate verifies the item was brought out of India and allows for duty-free re-importation upon return. https://www.customsmangalore.gov.in/
  • Certificates related to duty exemptions or specific programs: Indian Customs may issue various certificates for specific purposes. For instance, a duty exemption certificate might be used for goods purchased from a bonded warehouse for personal use. Another example is the Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) program, which offers benefits like faster clearance for certified businesses.

For a more precise answer, you’d need to know the specific context of the “Indian Custom Certificate” in question.

Here are some resources that might be helpful:

  • Central Board of Indirect Taxes & Customs (CBIC): This is the official website of the Indian Customs department. https://www.cbic.gov.in/
  • ICEGATE: This is the online portal of Indian Customs for trade facilitation.  https://www.icegate.gov.in/

The benefits you receive depend on the specific type of “Indian Custom Certificate” involved. Here’s a breakdown of the two possibilities we discussed earlier:

1. Export Certificate:

  • Smoother Re-importation: This certificate allows you to bring the declared high-value items back into India duty-free, as long as you haven’t modified them and they are for your personal use.
  • Simplified Process: Having the Export Certificate avoids the need to re-declare the items upon return and potentially pay duties if you couldn’t provide proof of them being taken out of India earlier.

2. Certificates for Specific Programs (e.g., AEO):

The benefits here can be significant for businesses involved in import and export. For example, the Authorized Economic Operator (AEO) program offers several advantages:

  • Faster Customs Clearance: AEO certified companies experience faster processing of their cargo, reducing delays and saving time.
  • Reduced Scrutiny: AEO certification signifies a history of compliance, leading to less frequent physical examination of goods by customs.
  • Priority Treatment: AEO shipments might be prioritized for processing during peak hours or congestion at customs.
  • Mutual Recognition: AEO certification can be recognized by other countries with similar programs, facilitating smoother customs clearance abroad.

In summary:

  • An Export Certificate simplifies re-importation of declared high-value personal items.
  • Certificates from specific programs (like AEO) offer significant benefits for businesses dealing with customs, such as faster clearance and reduced hassle.

[/av_textblock]

UN certificate is issued by the Indian Institute of Packaging (IIP). The term “UN certificate” can have two main interpretations in India, depending on the context:

  1. UN Mark Certificate for Packaging of Dangerous Goods:

    • This is the most likely meaning in the context of India.
    • Mandatory for manufacturers of packages used for transporting hazardous goods (by air or sea) from India.
    • Ensures the packaging meets specific safety standards as outlined by the United Nations (UN) for international transportation of dangerous goods.

  2. UN Certificate for Other Purposes (Less Likely):

    • In some contexts, a UN certificate might refer to a more generic document issued by the United Nations or a UN agency.
    • This is less likely in India, where the UN Mark Certificate for packaging dominates the meaning.

Here’s a breakdown of the UN Mark Certificate for Packaging of Dangerous Goods:

  • Purpose:
    • Guarantees that packaging for hazardous materials adheres to international regulations for safe transportation through various modes (road, rail, sea, and air).
    • Minimizes risks associated with transporting dangerous goods.
  • Issuing Authority:
    • The Indian Institute of Packaging (IIP) is the authorized body to issue UN Mark Certificates in India.
  • Obtaining the Certificate:
    • Packaging manufacturers need to apply to the IIP and undergo a testing process to ensure their packaging meets UN standards for specific hazard classes.
    • The IIP website likely has detailed information on the application process and relevant standards: https://www.iip-in.com/
  • Validity:
    • The UN Mark Certificate has a limited validity period, typically up to 18 months, subject to certain conditions being met by the manufacturer.

Additional Notes:

  • If you’re unsure about the specific type of UN certificate you’re referring to, consider the context in which you encountered the term. In India, it most likely refers to the UN Mark Certificate for packaging.
  • The IIP website or contacting them directly can provide further clarification and details about the process for obtaining a UN Mark Certificate.

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” element_template=” one_element_template=” av_uid=’av-l93xqjik’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]

We also have a product certificate (Licensed) in SONCAP (Standards Organisation of Nigeria Conformity Assessment Programme) for Nigeria.

The Standards Organisation of Nigeria Conformity Assessment Programme (SONCAP) is a mandatory program established by the Federal Government of Nigeria to ensure that all regulated products imported into the country meet Nigerian Industrial Standards (NIS) or other internationally accepted standards. The goal of SONCAP is to protect Nigerians from substandard and unsafe products, while also creating a level playing field for domestic manufacturers.

Standards Organisation of Nigeria Conformity Assessment Programme (SONCAP)
Here’s a summary of SONCAP:
  • Implemented by: Standards Organisation of Nigeria (SON)
  • Established: December 1, 2005
  • Purpose: To ensure the quality, safety and environmental protection of imported goods in Nigeria

Process for Compliance

Exporters who want to ship goods to Nigeria that are included in the SONCAP program need to undergo a conformity assessment process. This process is performed by independent firms that have been accredited by SON. There are three routes that may be applied, depending on the type, risk and impact on health, safety and the environment of the product:

  • Route A: This is for unregistered or unlicensed products. Each shipment must be inspected, sampled, tested and verified against applicable standards.
  • Route B: This is for products that are registered or licensed in Nigeria, but not in the country of export. The manufacturer’s quality management system is assessed and product samples are tested.
  • Route C: This is for products that are already certified to an equivalent international standard by an ISO/IEC 17025 accredited certification body. The product certificate is accepted based on verification of authenticity.

Benefits of SONCAP

  • Protects Nigerian consumers from substandard and unsafe products
  • Promotes fair competition between imported and domestic products
  • Boosts consumer confidence in imported goods
  • Facilitates trade by reducing delays at customs

If you are a business planning to export goods to Nigeria, it is important to understand the SONCAP requirements and ensure that your products comply. You can find more information about SONCAP on the website of the Standards Organisation of Nigeria https://son.gov.ng/.

The Licensed Product Certificate, abbreviated as PC3, is one of three certificates issued under the Standards Organisation of Nigeria Conformity Assessment Programme (SONCAP). SONCAP is a mandatory program that ensures the quality, health, safety, and environmental protection of products imported into Nigeria.

[SONCAP Certification Process]

Here’s a breakdown of the three certificates:

  • Product Certificate 1 (PC1): This is issued for unregistered low-risk products and is valid for a single shipment.
  • Product Certificate 2 (PC2): This is issued for registered low-risk products and is valid for multiple shipments within a one-year period.
  • Product Certificate 3 (PC3) Licensed: This is issued for high-risk products and requires a factory audit by a SONCAP Approved Body. PC3 certificates are valid for two years with bi-annual audits.

Obtaining a PC3 demonstrates a manufacturer’s commitment to quality and allows for faster customs clearance of goods in Nigeria.

If you are a manufacturer or exporter considering importing products into Nigeria, you should contact a SONCAP Approved Body to determine which certificate is required for your specific product and to initiate the certification process. You can find a list of SONCAP Approved Bodies on the Standards Organisation of Nigeria website https://son.gov.ng/son-product-certification/.

[/av_textblock]

    Umesh Balani  – Umesh is the founding Director of Rotomag, with over 30 years of experience in steering the business of electric drives, transmission systems and solar pumps. His leadership has been central to shaping the company’s strategic direction, operational excellence, and creating the footprint of our overseas business.   Throughout his career, he has combined technical insight with business acumen to identify new business opportunities, nurture the leadership teams, scale manufacturing capabilities, and align product development with evolving market needs.

 He is an Electronics Engineer from Birla Vishwakarma Mahavidyalay, SP University and an MBA graduate from S.P. Jain Institute of Management and Research, Mumbai.

 

Neelam Balani – is the Director of IT, Neelam is a seasoned operations and systems leader at Rotomag with 30 years of experience in research, education and business operations.  She has played a critical role in various functional areas at Rotomag including international sales, operations and quality.  During last 5 years she has been leading digital transformation of all our business as well as the operating systems and processes.

She is a MSc, M,Phil and PhD in Biotechnology as well as Bachelor’s in Business Administration from ICFAI, and has completed 1 year course in Operations Management from IIM-Lucknow.

 

Santanu Nath Bhaumik is a seasoned finance professional with over 25 years of experience in investment banking and corporate finance. He currently serves as Managing Director – Corporate Finance at Tangerine X.

Throughout his career, Santanu has held key leadership roles across a wide spectrum of financial institutions, showcasing deep expertise in strategic advisory, capital structuring, and corporate growth initiatives. His work has consistently focused on enabling businesses to navigate complex financial decisions and drive long-term value.

Santanu holds a postgraduate degree in Finance from the Institute of Management Technology (IMT), Ghaziabad, and a bachelor’sdegreeinMechanicalEngineering from Jadavpur University, Kolkata. His multidisciplinary academic foundation, coupled with his extensive industry experience, makes him a trusted and insightful advisor in the corporate finance landscape.

[/av_textblock]

A GST certificate, also known as a GST Registration Certificate, is an essential document for businesses registered under the Goods and Services Tax (GST) system in India.

Su-vastika Systems Private Limited GST registration number- 06ABACS5417M1Z6

  • GST Identification Number (GSTIN): This unique 15-digit alphanumeric code identifies the business for tax purposes and appears prominently on the certificate.
  • Business Name: The legal name under which the business operates will be displayed.
  • Trade Name (if applicable): If the business operates under a name different from its legal name, that will also be mentioned.
  • Registered Office Address: The official address where the business is registered will be included.

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” av_uid=’av-l93xqjik’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]

ISO 9001 Certificate of Su-vastika is ISO 9001: 2015, which is an international standard for Quality Management Systems (QMS) that outlines a framework for improving quality and a vocabulary of understanding for any organisation that wants to provide products and services that consistently meet the needs and expectations of customers and other relevant interested parties in the most efficient way possible.https://www.process.st/iso-9001-certification/

ISO 9001 certification is an international standard for defining a quality management system (QMS). It outlines various criteria (or standards) to define quality management principles, such as focusing on the customer, optimizing organisational leadership and management, improving and fine-tuning internal processes, and general continuous improvement methods.

Let’s break down each of the components of an ISO 9001 certification.

ISO

ISO, or the International Organization for Standardization, is an international standard-setting body based in Geneva, Switzerland. It comprises representatives from proprietary, industrial, and commercial standard organizations.

It’s the organization responsible for defining and implementing all of these standards.

The ISO itself doesn’t certify anything. Certifications are all done by third-party organizations.

9001

The number after ISO is the identifier of the standard. ISO 9001 is one of the most well-known ISO standards, defining criteria for general quality management systems.

ISO 9001 is part of the wider ISO 9000 family. All standards within ISO 9000 refer to quality management.

Essentially, this standard is designed to help organizations to be more efficient and more successfully meet the needs of their customers.

At least one million companies and organizations in 170+ countries are certified to ISO 9001.

Certification

ISO 9001 is also the only standard in the 9000 family that can be certified. This certification is not a requirement, and organizations can still implement the principles of ISO 9001 informally without getting certified.

Technically, only organizations can “get certified”. However, individuals can become Certified Lead Auditors, or Registrars, to perform audits on organizations.

Certification will generally cover the entire organization, focusing on specific facilities or departments tailored to improve performance.

Here’s a quick breakdown of the most important information about the ISO 9001 standard:

  • ISO 9001 is an international quality management system (QMS) standard.
  • ISO 9001 is part of the larger ISO 9000 family.
  • ISO 9001 is the only standard that can be certified. There is no such thing as “ISO Certification” or “ISO 9000 Certification”, only “ISO 9001 certification”.
  • Individuals cannot get ISO 9001 certified; rather, organizations or companies get certified.
  • However, individuals can get certified to become an ISO 9001 Certified Lead Auditor, enabling them to audit other companies.
  • The size of the organization does not matter. It could be one person or 100,000.
  • ISO 9001 is a general standard for quality management – it doesn’t matter what industry you’re in.
  • ISO 9001 is a process-focused standard, as opposed to a product-focused standard. It is not designed to define product quality (although if your process is good, your product should be too).

What does it mean to have an ISO 9001 certification?

Organizations that are ISO 9001 certified have successfully implemented a quality management system with all of the requirements detailed in the ISO 9001 standard.

Part of the ISO 9001 certification is ensuring improvement areas are identified and acted on. Organizations with ISO 9001 certification can be assumed to provide products and/or services that meet strict quality management standards.

Have a strong footing in international markets like Africa, the Middle East, and South-East Asia. Our sophisticated technology and progressive improvisation are set to capture markets in the rest of the World.

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” av_uid=’av-l93xqjik’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]
The MSME (Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises) certificate is a government-issued document identifying a business as an MSME. There are many benefits to obtaining an MSME certificate, including:

  • Access to government schemes and benefits: The government offers a variety of schemes and benefits specifically for MSMEs. These schemes can provide financial assistance, training, and other support to help MSMEs grow and succeed.
  • Reduced compliance costs: MSMEs with an MSME certificate are often exempt from certain compliance requirements, such as taxation and registration. This can save businesses time and money.
  • Improved access to finance: Banks and other financial institutions are more likely to lend to MSMEs with an MSME certificate. This is because the certificate assures that the business is legitimate and creditworthy.
  • Increased credibility and trust: An MSME certificate shows that a business has met certain criteria, such as minimum turnover and several employees. This can help increase the business’s credibility and trust with customers, suppliers, and other stakeholders.
  • Improved marketing opportunities: MSMEs with an MSME certificate are often eligible to participate in government-organized trade fairs and other marketing events. This can help businesses to reach new customers and grow their sales.

Overall, there are many benefits to obtaining an MSME certificate. It can help you to access government schemes and benefits, reduce compliance costs, improve access to finance, and increase your credibility and trust.https://eudyam.com/?msclkid=70ae012d60201bb3fc9b65e249ce79b4&utm_source=bing&utm_medium=cpc&utm_campaign=MSME%20Maharashtra&utm_term=msme%20company%20registration&utm_content=MSME%20General

Here are some additional benefits of obtaining an MSME certificate:

  • Eligibility for government tenders: Many government tenders are reserved for MSMEs. This means businesses with an MSME certificate will have a better chance of winning government contracts.
  • Increased brand awareness: An MSME certificate can help increase a business’s brand awareness. This shows that the business is legitimate and has met certain standards.
  • Improved employee morale: Employees may be more motivated to work for a business with an MSME certificate. This shows that the business is committed to growth and success.
  • Su-vastika having an MSME certificate, has earned the Export House Star certificate as well.https://suvastika.com/su-vastika-earns-star-export-house-certificate/

[/av_textblock]

[av_heading heading=’Startup certificate‘ tag=’h1′ style=’blockquote modern-quote modern-centered’ subheading_active=” show_icon=” icon=’ue800′ font=’entypo-fontello’ size=” av-medium-font-size-title=” av-small-font-size-title=” av-mini-font-size-title=” subheading_size=’24’ av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” icon_size=” av-medium-font-size-1=” av-small-font-size-1=” av-mini-font-size-1=” color=’custom-color-heading’ custom_font=’#004c86′ subheading_color=” seperator_color=” icon_color=” margin=” margin_sync=’true’ padding=’10’ icon_padding=’10’ headline_padding=” headline_padding_sync=’true’ link=’manually,http://’ link_target=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” av_uid=’av-l8ks9gw5′ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”][/av_heading]

[av_textblock textblock_styling_align=” textblock_styling=” textblock_styling_gap=” textblock_styling_mobile=” size=” av-medium-font-size=” av-small-font-size=” av-mini-font-size=” font_color=” color=” id=” custom_class=” template_class=” element_template=” one_element_template=” av_uid=’av-l93xqjik’ sc_version=’1.0′ admin_preview_bg=”]
Su-vastika Systems Private Limited is also certified by Startup India, which is a flagship initiative of the Government of India aimed at fostering the startup culture and creating a robust and inclusive environment for innovation and entrepreneurship in the country.

In India, the term “Startup Certificate” might not be the most precise wording. There isn’t a single certificate specifically called a “Startup Certificate.” However, there is a program called Startup India that recognizes startups and offers them various benefits. This recognition comes in the form of a DPIIT Certificate of Recognition for Startups.

Here’s a breakdown of the key points:

  • Startup India Recognition: This program by the Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) acknowledges businesses as startups.
  • DPIIT Certificate: Upon successful application and meeting eligibility criteria, a startup receives a DPIIT Certificate of Recognition for Startups. This doesn’t resemble a traditional certificate but is a digital record with a unique recognition number.
  • Benefits of Recognition: Holders of the DPIIT Certificate can avail themselves of various benefits offered by the Startup India initiative, including:
    • Tax exemptions for up to three years
    • Easier compliance with various regulations
    • Faster IPR processing
    • Access to government funding schemes

Who Can Apply:

  • Private Limited Companies
  • Limited Liability Partnerships (LLPs)
  • Registered Partnerships

Eligibility Criteria (broad guidelines):

  • The entity should be less than seven years old.
  • Turnover should be less than INR 100 crore in any of the previous financial years.
  • The entity should be working towards innovation, development of new products or processes, or creating a scalable business model with high potential for wealth or employment generation.

Obtaining the DPIIT Certificate:

The process typically involves submitting an online application through the Startup India portal along with supporting documents. The application is reviewed, and upon approval, the startup receives recognition and a unique recognition number.

The certificate you receive isn’t physically printed but a digital record with a recognition number. Officially called the DPIIT Certificate of Recognition for Startups, it unlocks a variety of benefits offered by the Startup India initiative. Here are some key advantages of obtaining this certificate:

  • Tax Exemptions: Qualifying startups can enjoy tax exemption on profits for up to three consecutive financial years out of their first ten years of operation. This benefit falls under Section 80 IAC of the Income Tax Act.

  • Reduced Compliance Burden: Startups with the certificate benefit from self-certification of compliance for six labor laws and three environmental laws. This simplifies the process and reduces administrative hassles. Additionally, for a period of five years following registration, startups are exempt from inspections under these labor laws.

  • Faster Patent Processing: The recognition expedites the processing of patent applications, saving valuable time for startups focusing on innovation.

  • Government Funding Schemes: The certificate opens doors to applying for various government funding schemes specifically designed to support startups. These schemes offer financial aid to help startups grow and develop.

  • Easier Public Procurement: Startup India certified businesses can participate in government tenders with relaxed norms compared to established companies. This facilitates participation in public procurement projects and increases potential business opportunities.

  • Reduced Capital Gains Tax: Investors in startups holding the DPIIT certificate may benefit from reduced capital gains tax on investments made in the startup through a Fund of Funds scheme.

  • Improved Credibility and Visibility: Having the recognition acts as a mark of legitimacy and demonstrates the government’s acknowledgement of the startup’s potential. This can enhance credibility and attract potential investors or partners.

  • Easier Winding Up: In case a startup decides to wind up its operations, the certificate allows for a more streamlined closure process compared to non-registered businesses.

Overall, the Startup India certificate offers a significant package of advantages that can be instrumental in a startup’s growth and success. It eases financial burdens, simplifies regulatory processes, opens doors to funding and opportunities, and enhances the startup’s overall standing in the business ecosystem.

Here are the resource for further information:

  • Startup India Website: https://www.startupindia.gov.in/

[/av_textblock]

You can display not only one Layerslider but multiple ones on every page.

title: About
content_html:
Su-vastika is a prominent Indian solar storage and power backup solutions company, founded in 2019. Based in Gurugram, India, they specialize in transitioning homes and businesses from traditional lead-acid batteries and polluting diesel generators to safer, greener, and more efficient Lithium-ion (LiFePO4) Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) and solar inverters.

Here is an overview of what makes Su-vastika stand out:

Key Offerings

Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS): Advanced, IoT-monitored lithium energy banks designed to completely eliminate the need for heavy diesel generators in factories, offices, and institutions.

Retrofit Lithium Batteries: Purpose-built, lightweight lithium batteries designed as a direct, plug-and-play replacement for traditional, bulky tubular lead-acid batteries in older inverters.

Solar Inverters: Smart, grid-tied and off-grid solar PCUs (Power Conditioning Units) that can manage and blend solar power, grid power, and battery storage.

Lift UPS Systems: Uninterruptible power solutions with specialized lithium battery options to keep elevators operating smoothly during sudden grid outages.

Smart Manufacturing: Su-vastika operates a state-of-the-art 5-acre facility with fully automated testing systems that log manufacturing data to the cloud, ensuring high quality and safety.
